generated_from: scripts/eval_detection.py + scripts/eval_attribution.py on the synthetic world (ingestion/synthetic.py), which is the only world where ground truth exists and accuracy can be scored.
why_synthetic: Real cities have no ground-truth emission inventory to score against. The synthetic world is deliberately adversarial: different dispersion physics than the scorer, sources that appear on no map, decoy sites that emit nothing, and a satellite blurred to its real ~5.5 km footprint.

detection.headline: Recall is reported BY WHAT THE INSTRUMENTS CAN PHYSICALLY SEE, not as one number.

tiers.note: Both appear on NO MAP AT ALL — unregistered. That is the real result.

NO₂ is a real combustion tracer, but the road network lifts it across the whole core, so a point source must out-shout its own neighbourhood. Hard, not impossible — on real Delhi this tier did surface industrial zones.
Coarse PM. It has no satellite tracer and it does not burn. Nothing we have sees it.

cell_precision.note: the shortfall is plume extent INSIDE correctly-found zones, not false accusations

unregistered.note: every source named was one that appears on no map

caveats: SMALL n. 2 detectable sources and 2 zones is not a rate. By our own principle 8, a clean number deserves suspicion before applause.

We previously reported 4/4 detection recall. It rested on SO₂ contrast, and real Sentinel-5P SO₂ over a city is noise (SNR 0.66–0.87, 49% of values negative). We deleted the channel and the number fell to 2/4. The instrument model had been kinder than the instrument.

'0 of 9 sources sit within 2 km of a monitor' is NOT a finding — it is an assumption of the world model. pick_station_cells() excludes each source's k=2 ring, so it is true ~99% of the time by construction. The empirical version, which owes nothing to our placement rule: an unbiased 12-monitor network catches a median of 1 source in 9.

Detection recall is invariant to where monitors sit (it reads satellite + FIRMS and never touches a station). Fusion LOSO is not, and moves with siting — as it must. That contrast is the evidence the headline is not an artefact of a rule we invented.
